title: Propegate exit code on Exec calls
body:
Signed-off-by: haircommander <pehunt@redhat.com>

Fixes: #402 

Returns Error code from a failed exec call to users terminal for debugging

comments.body:
Can one of the admins verify this patch?
I understand the following commands:
- `bot, add author to whitelist`
- `bot, test pull request`
- `bot, test pull request once`
@haircommander I need tests for this.  Could you cause an exec command to exit with a status.

Here's a simple bash script based on the original issue the fix was based on:

```
#!/bin/bash
podman run -d --name foo registry.access.redhat.com/rhel7/rhel:latest sleep 5m

podman exec foo /bin/false
echo $?

podman exec foo /bin/bash -c 'exit 32'
echo $?

podman exec foo /bin/bash -c 'pwd'
echo $?

```
Old output (omitting the result of the initial run):
```
exit status 1
125
exit status 32
125
/
0
```


New output (similarly omitting):
```
exit status 1
1
exit status 32
32
/
0
```
@haircommander Check out the files in libpod/src/github.com/projectatomic/libpod/test/e2e and please add to one or make a new one.
Just one or two to verify different exit codes should be fine
Haven't gotten Ginkgo working yet, so I'll tie this up tomorrow @mheon @rhatdan I wouldn't want to send bad test code....
Great job @haircommander.  Will wait for the tests.

@haircommander Looks like you screwed up your rebase.

You submitted a merge branch.
hip hip hooray for github... I haven't even finished this test yet
